rows.source_ref: loveevery1, "Another simple system - Time-Frame 15", ForexFactory thread 345586 (~2012, opening post + first 3 forum pages), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/345586-another-simple-system-time-frame-15
rows.concept: After the London open, take TDI green-crosses-yellow momentum entries in the direction of the 200-EMA trend, confirmed by a Heiken-Ashi close through the 10 EMA and a round-number bounce.
rows.eligibility_reason: Mechanizable core (TDI cross + 200/10 EMA trend filter + London-session window, 20-pip SL, TP 1:1 or trail on TDI cross-back, BE at +12) implementable in pure MQL5 on FX majors; no ML, has hard stop -> FTMO/DXZ compatible.
rows.notes: Newbie-oriented forum system with anecdotal '80% profitable' claims, no backtests, and heavy discretion (round-number 'bounce', 'momentum', 'supporting factors', flat-yellow-line skip) layered over the mechanical kernel. TDI = RSI + BB/MA-on-RSI (standard). Trend filter is hard: no shorts above 200 EMA, no longs below. Pairs traded: EURUSD, GBPUSD. Author cites a 4H variant (take crosses below/above the yellow line) and promises '2 more setups' that are never disclosed -> only one testable candidate. Session-structure FX is a wanted driver category, but the entry is essentially a well-trodden TDI/EMA momentum-continuation play; round-number bounce logic under-specified. Uses JF trading-time and round-number indicators + Heiken-Ashi candles.
